seoTitle: Port Gibbon vs Port Hughes — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Port Gibbon and Port Hughes. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: Port Gibbon vs Port Hughes: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Port Gibbon and Port Hughes.
heading: Port Gibbon vs Port Hughes — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Port Gibbon, South Australia and Port Hughes, South Australia. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Port Hughes has a lower overall climate risk score (30/100) compared to Port Gibbon (33/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Port Gibbon or Port Hughes safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Port Gibbon has a flood risk score of 29/100, while Port Hughes scores 31/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Port Gibbon scores 38/100 for bushfire risk. Port Hughes scores 17/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Port Gibbon or Port Hughes?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Port Hughes has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 0.38% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
